articles.title: Oil prices see largest two-day percentage gain in four months on U.S.-Iran fighting
articles.description: West Texas Intermediate and Brent crude’s front-month contracts climbed on Tuesday as investors worry about further de-escalation in the Middle East

articles.title: One of the world’s weakest currencies makes this Asian capital the world’s cheapest major city — for now
articles.description: After fifteen years of relative decline, the inexorably falling Japanese yen has made Tokyo one of the world’s cheapest major cities

articles.title: ‘Impeccably designed’: You can rent Orlando Bloom’s former Beverly Hills estate for just $31,000 a month
articles.description: Actor Orlando Bloom’s former Beverly Hills mansion is being offered up as a luxury rental, with a hefty asking price of $31,000 a month—with many of the Hollywood star’s custom upgrades still found throughout.

articles.title: The U.S. is maxing out its strategic oil reserves as Trump vows to control the Strait of Hormuz
articles.description: Strategic Petroleum Reserve stockpiles are precariously low and have been hit by major equipment failures, leaks and spills, according to a government report.

articles.title: Sandisk’s stock is plunging, but some analysts are getting even more bullish
articles.description: One new stock-price target implies nearly 85% upside from current levels.

articles.title: ‘It’s heartbreaking’: My brother claimed Social Security at 70. He died from cancer after one payment. Why wait to claim?
articles.description: “I’ve always been a little skeptical of the government’s encouragement to delay claiming benefits.”

articles.title: I’m 67 with a $140,000 pension. Should I wait until 70 to claim Social Security so my wife gets more?
articles.description: “When I pass, all my retirement income is reduced to $30,000 a year.”

articles.title: Why a borrowing binge by investors is a warning sign for the stock market
articles.description: Investors are increasingly borrowing to buy stocks, a reflection of greed in the stock market as they seek to amplify returns with margin debt. The growing pile of borrowed money has some on Wall Street nervous.

articles.title: SpaceX’s stock threatens to fall below the IPO price. Do investors face a ‘crisis’ if it does?
articles.description: The company has shed more than $800 billion in market value from its $2.67 trillion peak in less than a month.

articles.title: Lindsey Graham died from a rare heart condition. Here’s how to know if you’re at risk.
articles.description: Aortic dissections are extremely rare; however, people with a family history or who have been diagnosed with certain genetic syndromes have an elevated risk

articles.title: 'Funflation' hits home: Why staying in isn't the cost-saver it used to be
articles.description: Following a wave of price hikes, at-home pastimes like streaming movies or playing video games are pinching pocketbooks.

articles.title: India's inflation accelerates to 4.38% in June, exceeding forecasts
articles.description: Inflation has risen for the eighth straight month in India, as food and energy prices rise due to the Iran war and deficient rainfall.

articles.title: Job seekers giving up: Labor force participation rate falls to lowest in 50 years, outside of Covid era
articles.description: A drop in the unemployment rate helped provide some upside to what was an otherwise downbeat jobs report — but it was for all the wrong reasons.

articles.title: China consumer price growth weakens in June while producer inflation rises to near 4-year high

articles.title: Christine Lagarde leaves door open to early ECB exit, as she mulls French politics

articles.title: U.S. job creation cools in June with payrolls growth of just 57,000; unemployment rate at 4.2%
articles.description: Nonfarm payrolls were expected to rise by 115,000 in June and the unemployment rate to hold steady at 4.3%.

articles.title: Europe wants to rebalance trade with Beijing, but can't quit Chinese air conditioners

articles.title: Private payrolls rose by 98,000 in June, less than expected, ADP reports
